title: Trustworthy Notes
description: The mechanical encoding of METHODOLOGY §8. A notes-set extracted from one source page: terms (vocabulary), a separate evidence store (records stored once, referenced by id), statements (atomic notes that reference evidence), and relations (the reasoning links). A project-local SUPERSET of the pkit evidence-record shape (§9): same record concept, plus the structured source fields (page/stream/locator/bbox) the kit record lacks. Methodology is the source of truth; this schema changes only when METHODOLOGY does. Note: this schema enforces SHAPE only. Referential integrity (§7.4) and kind-aware traceability (§7.2) are code-enforced validity checks, not expressible here.

detected_language.description: The page's dominant language as a short ISO-ish code (e.g. 'en', 'cs', 'ja'), reported by the extractor as it reads the page (ADR-008, issue #115). A per-page provenance fact, like 'generation': captured free at extraction, carried verbatim through the anchor gate and compose, and rolled up to a per-doc source language for the advisory translate-offer gate. Optional and additive: notes made before this field read as 'unknown' (no schema_version bump — per-page notes are regenerable application output, not adopter state). It records the SOURCE language only; it never drives any §7 anchor check.

terms.description: The document-global term store (METHODOLOGY §4.1). In a multi-file run this is a separate document-level artifact that page/chapter notes-sets reference; a single self-contained notes-set may inline it here. Term identity is reconciled at compose.

evidence.description: The evidence store. Records are stored once here and referenced by id from statements (METHODOLOGY §4.4).

slug.description: Kebab-case stable identifier (used where the kind isn't fixed, e.g. relation endpoints which may be a statement or a term).

source.description: The origin document and the span these notes cover. 'scope' is page (intermediate, one per page) or chapter (the composed deliverable) — METHODOLOGY §4.6. Chapter scope is reserved (chapter notes not produced yet).

document.description: Human-readable citation of the source document.

page_label.description: The printed page number a scholar would cite (e.g. '3'). Page scope.

page_index.description: 0-based PDF page index we resolve evidence against (e.g. 13). Required for page scope.

page_range.description: [start_index, end_index] of 0-based PDF pages composed into this chapter. Required for chapter scope.

allOf.comment: page scope (explicit or default-by-omission) requires page_index.

generation.description: The extractor settings that PRODUCED these notes — the generation provenance (ADR-007, issue #98). Distinct from the eval instrument fingerprint (how a score was measured); this records how the notes being scored were made. Optional and additive: notes generated before this field read as 'unknown' generation, never a contradiction (no schema_version bump — per-page notes are regenerable application output, not adopter state). 'eval' rolls these per-page settings up to a per-doc label and flags a doc whose pages disagree as mixed/contaminated.

model.description: The model id that extracted the page (e.g. the Sonnet model string).

effort.description: The thinking effort the extractor ran at (e.g. 'low', 'medium', 'high').

max_tokens.description: The max-tokens budget the extraction ran under.

term.description: A named concept/word the document uses. NOT an assertion (METHODOLOGY §4.1).

label.description: The term as it reads, e.g. 'annuity contract (sX n sanx)'.

evidence.description: One verifiable pointer into the source, stored once and referenced by id (METHODOLOGY §4.4, §6). Discriminated by 'kind'. Only 'text' is fully built; 'figure'/'table' are reserved.

kind.description: What sort of source evidence this is (METHODOLOGY §4.4).

excerpt.description: Verbatim text copied from the source stream. Required for kind=text.

source.description: Which extracted stream to resolve against. Required for kind=text.

script.description: Writing system of a text excerpt (METHODOLOGY §4.4). Open vocabulary; common values: 'latin' (default), 'egyptian-transliteration'. Transliteration signs are restored to Unicode at ingest (MdC: T=ṯ, H=ḥ, …).

locator.description: Footnote marker (e.g. '11'), figure/table number, or section label.

caption.description: Verbatim caption for a figure/table; verified as text when present.

bbox.description: Region [x0, top, x1, bottom] on the page for a figure/table.

page_label.description: Printed page label; omit to inherit from source.page_label.

page_index.description: 0-based PDF page; omit to inherit from source.page_index. Set only for cross-page evidence.

allOf.comment: kind=text (explicit or default-by-omission) requires a verbatim excerpt and a source stream (METHODOLOGY §6).

statement.description: An atomic note: one piece of knowledge, typed, grounded by >=1 evidence reference (METHODOLOGY §4.2, §5).

type.description: What KIND of knowledge this is (METHODOLOGY §5, type axis).

basis.description: WHO asserts it and on what grounds (METHODOLOGY §5, basis axis). 'author-data' is a finding; 'reported' is a reported claim. Omit to mean 'author'.

text.description: Our wording of the idea; may paraphrase. The verbatim copy lives in the referenced evidence, never here.

terms.description: ids of terms this statement is about (must be 't-'). Existence is a code-enforced check (§7.4).

evidence.description: ids into the evidence store (must be 'e-'). At least one is mandatory (§7.1). Existence is a code-enforced check (§7.4).

relation.description: A labelled link that keeps the reasoning intact (METHODOLOGY §4.3, §5).
